hypotheses.label: ngram-4 feedback floods the corpus with near-duplicate shallow-tokenizer seeds, starving energy from the rare deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild path that naive reaches
hypotheses.prediction: G1: naive_ngram4 corpus is inflated with shallow-gram entries (corpus_size_ratio of ngram4/naive >> 1) AND naive reaches strictly deeper into the parser call chain (depth_reach_inv: naive hits xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild true-side, ngram4 stays at xmlParseName/xmlParseCharData). A non-saturation mechanism (e.g. ngram4 simply never mutating the right '&...;' bytes) would predict comparable corpus sizes with no shallow-gram enrichment, so the saturation reading is falsifiable and discriminating.
hypotheses.non_decidable_reason: data_realities forbid the measurement for this branch: (1) on-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl, harfbuzz, openthread, sqlite3 -- libxml2 has none, so corpus_queue:corpus_size_ratio and corpus_queue:depth_reach cannot be computed; (2) the mechanism is purely corpus-SCALE / energy-allocation (shallow-gram flooding) which has no fixed-offset value or structural-token signature any seed-set descriptor could capture for a single branch; (3) n=1 branch gives no cross-branch separation to backstop a threshold. No real-data measurement in the registry or vocabulary can discriminate saturation-starvation from plain non-mutation for this branch.

data_realities_respected: libxml2 not in on-disk-corpus set (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3) -> corpus_size_ratio + depth_reach both INFEASIBLE here
no seed_lineage 'ngram event' proposed (ngram is a FEEDBACK, untagged in mutation_op, analogous to value_profile invisibility)
no byte_freq_ratio proposed (gate is presence/non-null structural, not a fixed-offset value gate; different-sized seeds)
notes: target=libxml2. SINGLE branch, SINGLE mechanism (ngram shallow-corpus saturation starving the deep xmlParseReference->xmlAddChild null-parent path; naive's single-edge MaxMapFeedback saturates shallow tokenizers fast and reallocates energy deeper). The proposed NEW descriptors (corpus_queue:corpus_size_ratio and corpus_queue:depth_reach, both registry_tool:null) would be the right measurements IN PRINCIPLE -- inflated ngram4 corpus + inverted depth reach -- but data_realities forbid them: libxml2 has no on-disk corpus/queue, and the mechanism is purely corpus-scale with no value/structural seed-set signature to fall back on. With n=1 there is also no cross-branch threshold support. Caveat: were libxml2 corpora later instrumented, this becomes decidable via the two descriptors above; until then it is non-discriminable. Marked decidable:false -> arbiter routes to fallback inconclusive.
